title: Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
text: Downloads AccountabilitySummative sheet redesigned 2024-25 School Performance Reports. school-level ESSA summative accountability record: indicator (ELA/Math proficiency, ELA/Math growth, 4/5/6-year graduation, progress toward English language proficiency, chronic absenteeism, high-school persistence) reports actual performance, weighted indicator score, indicator's weight, rolls summative_score summative_rating.

title: Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
text: end_year school year. 2025 (SY2024-25) later supported.

title: Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
text: Data frame entity identifiers, school_year, subgroup, title_i,   school_configuration, per-indicator *_actual_performance,   *_indicator_score, *_weight columns, summative_score,   summative_rating, aggregation flags.

title: Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
text: sheet exists School database, function always reads school-level data (level argument). Performance, score, weight, summative columns returned numeric (percent signs stripped, "n/"/suppressed cells set NA); title_i school_configuration kept labels. subgroup standardized via SPR subgroup cleaner. Supported years: end_year >= 2025.

title: Fetch ESSA Summative Accountability Scores — fetch_spr_accountability_summative
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # School-level summative scores summ <- fetch_spr_accountability_summative(2025)  # Lowest summative scores among schoolwide (total population) rows library(dplyr) fetch_spr_accountability_summative(2025) %>%   filter(subgroup == "total population", !is.na(summative_score)) %>%   slice_min(summative_score, n = 10) %>%   select(district_name, school_name, summative_score, summative_rating)  # ELA vs Math proficiency contribution to the score fetch_spr_accountability_summative(2025) %>%   filter(subgroup == "total population") %>%   select(school_name, ela_proficiency_indicator_score,          math_proficiency_indicator_score) } # }

title: Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
text: Downloads ESSA accountability long-term-goal target sheets redesigned 2024-25 School Performance Reports. sheet reports, per entity student group, indicator's actual performance annual target (state standard) , applicable, federal long-term goal.

title: Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
text: end_year school year. 2025 (SY2024-25) later supported. indicator One "proficiency" (default), "growth", "graduation", "elp", "absenteeism", "persistence". level One "school" "district". "school" returns school-level data; "district" returns district state-level data.

title: Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
text: Data frame columns: end_year, county_id, county_name,   district_id, district_name, school_id, school_name, school_year,   subgroup, indicator, measure, indicator_performance,   indicator-specific target columns described , target_status (except   "persistence"), aggregation flags (is_state, is_county,   is_district, is_school, is_charter, is_charter_sector, is_allpublic).

title: Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
text: indicator argument selects one six target sheets, share common backbone (entity identifiers, school_year, subgroup, measure, indicator_performance) differ target columns: "proficiency" – ProficiencyTargets: ELA Math     NJSLA proficiency. Columns: annual_target, long_term_goal,     target_status. "growth" – GrowthTargets: ELA Math median student     growth. Columns: state_standard_growth, target_status. "graduation" – GraduationTargets: 4-, 5-, 6-year     graduation rates. Columns: annual_target, long_term_goal,     target_status. "elp" – ProgresstowardELPTargets: progress toward     English language proficiency. Columns: annual_target,     long_term_goal, target_status. "absenteeism" – ChronicAbsenteeismTargets: chronic     absenteeism. Columns: target_state_average, target_status. "persistence" – HSPersistenceTargets: high-school     persistence. target/status columns (performance ). measure holds sheet's breakdown (e.g. "ELA Proficiency" vs. "Math Proficiency", "4-Year Graduation" vs. "5-Year Graduation"). indicator added constant column equal requested value results several indicators can row-bound told apart. Value columns (indicator_performance, annual_target, long_term_goal, state_standard_growth, target_state_average) returned numeric; suppressed -N-size cells become NA. target_status kept raw status label (e.g. "Met Target", "Met CI", "N-Size"). Supported years: end_year >= 2025 (redesigned SY2024-25 SPR). Earlier databases include sheets.

title: Fetch ESSA Long-Term-Goal Targets — fetch_spr_essa_targets
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # School-level proficiency targets (default indicator) prof <- fetch_spr_essa_targets(2025)  # District/state-level graduation targets grad <- fetch_spr_essa_targets(2025, indicator = "graduation", level = "district")  # Which schools missed their ELA proficiency long-term goal? library(dplyr) fetch_spr_essa_targets(2025, indicator = "proficiency") %>%   filter(is_school, subgroup == "total population", measure == "ELA Proficiency") %>%   filter(target_status == "Did Not Meet Target") %>%   select(district_name, school_name, indicator_performance, long_term_goal) } # }

title: Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways

title: Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways
Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways

title: Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways
text: Data frame entity identifiers, school_year, subject, four   pathway percentage columns, aggregation flags.

title: Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways
text: Pathways (columns, percentage 0-100 scale): statewide_assessment – met via statewide NJSLA/NJGPA     assessment. substitute_competency_test – met via approved substitute     competency test (e.g. SAT, ACT, PSAT, ASVAB). portfolio_appeals – met via portfolio appeals process. alternate_requirements_in_iep – met via alternate     requirements specified student's IEP. Percentages returned numeric (suppressed cells become NA). Supported years: 2018-2022, 2024, 2025. GraduationPathways sheet present SPR databases (absent SY2016-17 SY2022-23 databases, therefore error). 2024-25 redesign columns named slightly differently (ELA/Math, PARCCAssessment/StatewideAssessment, SubstituteCompetency, PortfolioAppealsProcess, AlternateReqIEP); function harmonizes redesigned names uppercases subject label. COVID-era executive-order waiver column present 2020 2021 sheets part four-pathway schema dropped.

title: Fetch Graduation Pathways — fetch_spr_grad_pathways
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # School-level graduation pathways gp <- fetch_spr_grad_pathways(2025)  # The same pathway mix back to SY2017-18 gp_2018 <- fetch_spr_grad_pathways(2018)  # Statewide ELA pathway mix library(dplyr) fetch_spr_grad_pathways(2025, level = "district") %>%   filter(is_state, subject == "ELA") %>%   select(statewide_assessment, substitute_competency_test,          portfolio_appeals, alternate_requirements_in_iep)  # Schools leaning hardest on portfolio appeals for Math fetch_spr_grad_pathways(2025) %>%   filter(is_school, subject == "Math") %>%   slice_max(portfolio_appeals, n = 10) %>%   select(district_name, school_name, portfolio_appeals) } # }

title: Fetch NAEP Achievement Results — fetch_spr_naep
text: NAEP state/national summary table county/district/school breakdown, function returns CDS identifiers aggregation flags. state_nation column distinguishes "New Jersey" "Nation"; test_year NAEP administration year (NAEP given periodically, multiple years appear). four achievement-level columns returned numeric 0-100 scale. Supported years: end_year >= 2017. Always reads District/State database (School database NAEP sheet). 2024-25 redesign sheet used leaner layout (Year, Test, Grade four achievement levels) student-group breakdown; function maps redesigned shape: Year -> test_year, Test -> subject, legacy "State (NJ)" label normalized "New Jersey", student_group set constant "Students" (legacy sheet reports -students summary ; per-subgroup breakdown added 2024-25).

title: Fetch NAEP Achievement Results — fetch_spr_naep
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # NAEP results as published in the 2024-25 SPR naep <- fetch_spr_naep(2025)  # NAEP as published in an earlier SPR (all-students summary only) naep_2024 <- fetch_spr_naep(2024)  # New Jersey vs. the nation, Grade 4 Math, most recent administration library(dplyr) fetch_spr_naep(2025) %>%   filter(subject == "Mathematics", grade == "4",          student_group == "All Students") %>%   filter(test_year == max(test_year)) %>%   select(state_nation, below_basic, basic, proficient, advanced) } # }

what: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
title: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
text: One year NJ DOE K-12 state aid, broken per district category (equalization, educational adequacy, school choice, transportation, special education, security, adjustment, vocational expansion stabilization, military impact) plus year totals. state-aid (revenue subsidy) counterpart spending data fetch_tges.

title: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
text: end_year school year (end academic year): 2025-26 year (state FY2026) end_year = 2026.

title: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
text: tibble one row per district per aid category:   county_name, district_id, district_name,   end_year, is_state, is_district, aid_category,   is_aid_category, amount, raw label aid_category_raw.

title: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
text: Returned long, one row per district per category. is_aid_category marks individual aid categories (TRUE) versus year totals difference columns (FALSE); filter is_aid_category clean categorical breakdown. Category names normalized across years (e.g. "Choice Aid" "School Choice Aid" become school_choice_aid; "Special Education Categorical Aid" becomes special_education_aid). Data come NJ DOE Office School Finance "District Details" workbook published Governor's Budget Message. appropriated / proposed aid figures, audited expenditures. Note particular transportation_aid formula subsidy typically far district's actual transportation cost. Valid end_year values 2019 later. year's workbook located trying current-year direct URL first, archived per-year zip bundle.

title: Fetch NJ K-12 State Aid by District and Category — fetch_state_aid
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # All categories for one year fetch_state_aid(2026) %>%   filter(is_district, is_aid_category) %>%   select(district_name, aid_category, amount)  # Transportation aid, biggest recipients fetch_state_aid(2026) %>%   filter(is_district, aid_category == "transportation_aid") %>%   arrange(desc(amount)) %>%   select(district_name, amount)  # One district's aid mix fetch_state_aid(2026) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570", is_aid_category) %>%   select(aid_category, amount) } # }

title: Rank entities by achievement gap within peer group — gap_percentile_rank
text: df Output calculate_subgroup_gap() dataframe gap column gap_col Character. column containing gap values. Default "metric_gap". peer_type Character. Peer group type. See define_peer_group(). year_col Character. Year column name. Default "end_year". smaller_is_better Logical. TRUE (default), smaller gaps get higher percentile ranks. Set FALSE larger gaps preferred.

title: Track achievement gap trends over time — gap_trajectory
text: df Dataframe subgroups metrics multiple years metric_col Character. metric track. subgroup_a Character. Reference subgroup. subgroup_b Character. Comparison subgroup. year_col Character. Year column. Default "end_year". entity_cols Character vector. Columns identifying entity track.

title: Track achievement gap trends over time — gap_trajectory
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Track Newark's Black-White grad rate gap over time grate %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   gap_trajectory(     metric_col = "grad_rate",     subgroup_a = "white",     subgroup_b = "black"   ) } # }

title: Identify charter host districts — id_charter_hosts
text: df host district id/name plus share column (1.0   single-host charters non-charters; fractional apportioned   multi-campus charters) is_apportioned logical flag. Rows   apportioned charters duplicated, one per host city.

title: Identify charter host districts — id_charter_hosts
text: Multi-campus charters. NJ DOE assigns one district_id per charter report charter campuses separately, charters operate campuses one host city single district_id (e.g. M.E.T.S. Charter School, district 6068, ran Jersey City campus later Newark campus). , charter_host_apportionment table splits charter's NJ-reported totals across host cities share fraction sums 1.0 per district_id per end_year. charter apportionment entry relevant year, single input row expanded one row per host city, host columns overwritten apportionment table, share set accordingly downstream aggregations can multiply summed counts share summing. charter total preserved exactly. apportioned host assignment charters explicit, documented apportionment real NJ-reported totals (50/50 METS split PLACEHOLDER), never NJ-reported campus count. See charter_host_apportionment.

title: Identify Focus Schools — identify_focus_schools
text: Data frame focus schools including: original columns input data focus_level - Categorized support level: "Comprehensive Support Improvement" - Lowest performing 5 "Targeted Support Improvement" - Underperforming subgroups "Support" - identification categories Schools sorted focus level (Comprehensive first) alphabetically   district school name. Schools without support identification excluded.

title: Identify Focus Schools — identify_focus_schools
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get ESSA status data essa <- fetch_essa_status(2024)  # Identify focus schools focus <- identify_focus_schools(essa)  # View comprehensive support schools focus %>%   dplyr::filter(focus_level == "Comprehensive Support and Improvement") %>%   dplyr::select(district_name, school_name, category_of_identification)  # Focus on specific year focus_2023 <- identify_focus_schools(essa, end_year = 2023) } # }

what: Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
title: Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
text: Reshapes per-category TGES indicator tables one row per district-year major spending category per-pupil-dollar column, plus category's share budgetary per-pupil cost. backbone "dollars classroom" composition-drift analysis. Categories pulled table code (robust label changes): budgetary_pp (CSG1) – budgetary per-pupil cost (share denominator) classroom (CSG2), support_services (CSG6),         administration (CSG8), plant_ops (CSG10),         food_service (CSG12), extracurricular (CSG13),         equipment (CSG15) total_pp (CSG1AA) – total per-pupil expenditures, available Shares category divided budgetary_pp. guaranteed sum 1: categories standard TGES reporting buckets, strict partition, (food, extracurricular) sit outside budgetary per-pupil definition.

title: Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
text: tges Output fetch_tges() (one year) fetch_many_tges() (several years). years Optional numeric vector. Keep end_year values. calc_type Optional character. Keep calc type, e.g. "Actuals" "Budgeted".

title: Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
text: tibble entity columns (county_name, district_id,   district_name, group), end_year, calc_type,   per-pupil category columns, matching *_share columns.

title: Build a per-pupil spending composition table — tges_composition
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One year comp <- tges_composition(fetch_tges(2024))  # Classroom share for Newark, actuals only library(dplyr) tges_composition(fetch_many_tges(2020:2024), calc_type = "Actuals") %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, classroom, budgetary_pp, classroom_share)  # Lowest classroom-share districts in 2024 tges_composition(fetch_tges(2024), calc_type = "Budgeted") %>%   arrange(classroom_share) %>%   select(district_name, classroom_share, administration_share) %>%   head(10) } # }

what: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
title: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
text: district's budget move, move rank peers? takes spending-share composition two years returns drift (end minus start, share points) tracked category, ranks one chosen drift (classroom share default) within peer group. output "Newark moved 4 points classroom plant O&M, 2nd-largest classroom-share decline DFG " finding.

title: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
code: tges_composition_drift(   tges,   start_year = NULL,   end_year = NULL,   shares = c("classroom_share", "administration_share", "support_services_share",     "plant_ops_share"),   rank_on = "classroom_share",   peer = c("tges_group", "dfg", "county", "statewide"),   calc_type = "Budgeted",   dfg_revision = 2000 )

title: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
text: tges Output fetch_many_tges() (multi-year). start_year, end_year Numeric. Endpoints. Default: min max present. shares Character vector share columns tges_composition() compute drift . Default classroom / administration / support_services / plant_ops shares. rank_on Character. share's drift percentile-rank within peer group. Default "classroom_share". peer Character. Peer group. One "tges_group" (default), "dfg", "county", "statewide". calc_type Character. Composition calc type. Default "Budgeted". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision peer = "dfg". Default 2000.

title: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
text: per-district tibble: entity columns, peer_group,   start_year, end_year, {share}_start,   {share}_end, {share}_drift triple per tracked share,   rank columns drift_rank, drift_n, drift_percentile   rank_on (computed signed drift, larger positive drift   ranks higher).

title: Ranked composition-share drift versus peers — tges_composition_drift
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  drift <- tges_composition_drift(fetch_many_tges(2019:2024), peer = "dfg")  # biggest classroom-share declines among DFG A drift %>%   arrange(classroom_share_drift) %>%   select(district_name, classroom_share_start, classroom_share_end,          classroom_share_drift, drift_percentile) %>%   head(10) } # }

what: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
text: spending gaps within peer group closing widening? classic public-finance convergence test, pointed school dollars: regresses district's growth per-pupil spend starting level, within peer group. negative slope (beta) means low-spenders grew faster group converging; positive slope means high-spenders pulled ahead (divergence).

title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
code: tges_convergence(   tges,   metric_col = "Per Pupil costs",   table = "CSG1",   peer = c("tges_group", "dfg", "county", "statewide"),   start_year = NULL,   end_year = NULL,   calc_type = "Budgeted",   dfg_revision = 2000 )

title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
text: tges Output fetch_many_tges() (multi-year). metric_col Character. Numeric column track. Default "Per Pupil costs". table Character. TGES table code carrying metric_col. Default "CSG1" (budgetary per-pupil cost). peer Character. Peer group. One "tges_group" (default), "dfg", "county", "statewide". start_year, end_year Numeric. Endpoints. Default: min max end_year present. calc_type Character. Calc type keep. Default "Budgeted". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision peer = "dfg". Default 2000.

title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
text: per-district tibble: entity columns, peer_group,   start_year, end_year, start_value, end_value,   log_start_value, growth, broadcast group statistics   beta, beta_pvalue, r_squared, n_districts,   converging (beta < 0 beta_pvalue < 0.05).

title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
text: Needs multi-year input (fetch_many_tges()). district function takes metric start_year end_year, drops one value per district-year, computes annualized log growth \((\ln v_{end} - \ln v_{start}) / (end - start)\). Within peer group fits growth ~ log(start_value) OLS (requires >= 4 districts endpoints starting-level variation). slope stats broadcast onto every district row group, frame plots convergence scatter (log_start_value vs growth) reports group beta. one peer group's headline, take distinct(peer_group, beta, beta_pvalue, r_squared, n_districts, converging).

title: Beta-convergence of spending across a peer group — tges_convergence
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  conv <- tges_convergence(fetch_many_tges(2015:2024), peer = "dfg")  # one row per peer group: is each DFG converging on spend? conv %>% distinct(peer_group, beta, beta_pvalue, converging, n_districts) } # }

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier — tges_efficiency
text: Joins TGES per-pupil spend metric outcome percentile labels district's spend-vs-outcome position. comparative fiscal analyst's headline product, literal answer MarGrady brief's open "cost-effectiveness" question: outcomes rose, cost, efficient relative peers? Spend ranked within chosen peer group via tges_percentile_rank(). outcome percentile supplied caller (e.g. grate_percentile_rank() parcc_percentile_rank()), outcome metric peer definition stay caller's choice. efficiency residual vertical distance per-year regression outcome percentile spend percentile: positive means outcome spending predict.

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier — tges_efficiency

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier — tges_efficiency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Graduation outcomes ranked within DFG A, joined to per-pupil spend grate <- fetch_grad_rate(2023, methodology = "4 year") %>%   add_dfg() %>%   filter(dfg == "A", is_district, subgroup == "total") %>%   grate_percentile_rank(peer_type = "dfg")  spend <- fetch_tges(2024)$CSG1 %>%   filter(calc_type == "Actuals", end_year == 2023)  tges_efficiency(   spend, grate,   outcome_percentile_col = "grad_rate_percentile",   spend_peer = "dfg" ) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570")  # The "watch" quadrant: high spend, low outcome eff <- tges_efficiency(spend, grate,   outcome_percentile_col = "grad_rate_percentile") eff %>% filter(grepl("watch", quadrant)) } # }

what: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
title: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
text: NJ's headline comparative measure, Budgetary Per Pupil Cost (CSG1), deliberately excludes long list spending, notably state-paid -behalf TPAF pension, post-retirement medical, social security contributions, law paid state never appear district's budget.  helper joins Total Spending Detail workbook (2024+ TGES table) CSG1 can see, per district-year, every component sits budgetary cost total spending.

title: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
text: tges Output fetch_tges() fetch_many_tges() 2024+ guide. years Optional numeric vector. Keep end_year values. reliable_max_sent_share Numeric [0, 1]; maximum sent-pupil share per-pupil differences treated reliable. Default 0.02.

title: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
text: tibble one row per district-year: entity columns,   end_year, budgetary_pp, six Detail components,   total_spending_pp, excluded_total_pp, gce_excess_pp,   enrollment_plus_sent, budgetary_denom, sent_pupil_share,   logical residual_reliable.

title: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
text: Detail workbook splits Total Spending Per Pupil six components sum published total; helper returns clean per-pupil columns: general_current_expense_pp – general-fund current expense     (-behalf TPAF, transportation, tuition, judgments live) capital_outlay_pp, grants_entitlements_pp,     food_services_pp, debt_service_local_pp,     debt_service_sda_pp – five components computes two differences budgetary per-pupil cost: excluded_total_pp = total_spending_pp - budgetary_pp.     full wedge everything excluded budgetary     figure: -behalf TPAF pension/PRM/social security plus     transportation, capital outlay, grants/entitlements, food service, tuition,     debt service, judgments. gce_excess_pp = general_current_expense_pp - budgetary_pp.     narrower residual: general-fund items excluded budgetary cost, .e.     roughly transportation + -behalf TPAF + tuition + judgments. Neither difference isolates pension.  public TGES file breaks -behalf TPAF line; buried inside General Current Expense alongside transportation tuition.  itemized per-district pension figure exists NJDOE's login-gated AudSum submission.  Treat gce_excess_pp upper bound pension, measurement . Denominator caveat.  Budgetary per-pupil cost divides resident enrollment; Detail per-pupil amounts divide enrollment plus sent pupils.  districts educate pupils two agree within ~1%, sending districts (including big cities place special-ed vocational pupils district) denominators diverge per-pupil subtraction breaks .  sent_pupil_share reports (enrollment_plus_sent - resident_enrollment) / enrollment_plus_sent, residual_reliable TRUE share reliable_max_sent_share.  Filter residual_reliable reading anything gce_excess_pp. Total Spending Detail tables ship 2024 guide onward, needs fetch_tges(2024) later (guide carries two prior fiscal years).

title: What the budgetary per-pupil figure leaves out (incl. on-behalf TPAF pension) — tges_excluded_costs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # All excluded-cost components for the latest two fiscal years tges_excluded_costs(fetch_tges(2025)) %>%   select(district_name, end_year, budgetary_pp, total_spending_pp,          excluded_total_pp, gce_excess_pp)  # Only districts where the residual is denominator-reliable (self-contained), # ranked by the general-fund excess (~ transportation + on-behalf TPAF) tges_excluded_costs(fetch_tges(2025)) %>%   filter(residual_reliable, end_year == 2024) %>%   arrange(desc(gce_excess_pp)) %>%   select(district_name, budgetary_pp, gce_excess_pp, sent_pupil_share)  # Track one district's excluded-cost wedge across guides tges_excluded_costs(fetch_many_tges(2024:2025)) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, budgetary_pp, gce_excess_pp, excluded_total_pp) } # }

what: ESSER-cliff exposure: did recurring spending ride one-time federal money? — tges_federal_exposure
title: ESSER-cliff exposure: did recurring spending ride one-time federal money? — tges_federal_exposure

text: tges Output fetch_many_tges() (multi-year) fetch_tges(). baseline_years Numeric vector pre-pandemic years. Default: present years <= 2020. esser_years Numeric vector ESSER-window years. Default 2021:2024. bump_threshold Numeric. Minimum federal-share increase (0-1) flag. Default 0.03 (3 share points). growth_threshold Numeric. Minimum per-pupil spending growth flag. Default 0 (growth).

text: One row per district baseline/peak/bump/growth columns   cliff_exposure flag.

title: ESSER-cliff exposure: did recurring spending ride one-time federal money? — tges_federal_exposure
text: Requires multi-year revenue series (pass fetch_many_tges() output spanning baseline ESSER years). district: baseline_federal_share: mean federal share     baseline_years. peak_federal_share: max federal share esser_years. federal_bump: peak - baseline (share points, 0-1). baseline_pp / peak_pp: mean baseline max ESSER-window     total spending per pupil. pp_growth: peak_pp / baseline_pp - 1. cliff_exposure: TRUE federal_bump >=     bump_threshold pp_growth > growth_threshold – district     grew operating spend federal-revenue surge. screen, finding: see whether district reserved federal money one-time uses. surfaces look .

title: ESSER-cliff exposure: did recurring spending ride one-time federal money? — tges_federal_exposure
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  tg <- fetch_many_tges(2018:2025)  tges_federal_exposure(tg) %>%   filter(cliff_exposure) %>%   arrange(desc(federal_bump)) %>%   select(district_name, baseline_federal_share, peak_federal_share,          federal_bump, pp_growth) } # }

Composition report year anchor . Default: latest present. features Character vector feature columns match . Default c("ade", "budgetary_pp", "classroom_share", "administration_share", "local_share", "state_share"). numeric column assembled frame allowed (e.g. total_pp, federal_share, support_services_share, plant_ops_share). calc_type Character. Composition calc type. Default "Budgeted". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision reported dfg column.

title: Find a district's data-driven structural peers — tges_find_peers
text: Features assembled per district latest available report source (composition chosen year, recent CSG1AA enrollment, recent VITSTAT revenue mix), feature z-scored across districts complete data. ade (enrollment) log-transformed scaling heavy-tailed. Distance Euclidean norm scaled features; focal district must complete feature vector. Zero-variance features dropped warning. dfg reported context part distance.

title: Find a district's data-driven structural peers — tges_find_peers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Newark's data-driven fiscal twins peers <- tges_find_peers(fetch_tges(2024), district_id = "3570") peers %>% select(district_name, dfg, distance, ade, budgetary_pp, local_share)  # Use them as the peer set for an honest rank twin_ids <- peers$district_id fetch_tges(2024)$CSG1 %>%   tges_percentile_rank(peer = "custom", custom_ids = twin_ids) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(`Per Pupil costs`, peer_percentile, peer_n) } # }

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier
text: tges_efficiency() labels quadrant, computes proper efficiency score free-disposal-hull frontier. district finds peer achieves least outcome least spend, reports ratio (0-1, 1 = frontier) plus reference district dollars overspent relative . answers MarGrady brief's open cost-effectiveness question number, quadrant: "Bayonne hits graduation rate $2,100/pupil less; 0.81 efficiency."

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier
text: spend_df tidied TGES table (one report year, one calc_type) carrying district_id spend_col. outcome_df district-level frame district_id, year column, outcome_col (higher = better). spend_col Character. Spend column spend_df. Default "Per Pupil costs". outcome_col Character. Outcome column outcome_df. peer Character. Peer group frontier. One "tges_group" (default), "dfg", "county", "statewide". year_col Character. Year column, present frames. Default "end_year". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision peer = "dfg". Default 2000.

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier
text: tibble: entity columns, peer_group, year, spend,   outcome, efficiency_score, on_frontier,   reference_district_id, reference_district_name,   reference_spend, excess_spend.

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier
text: free-disposal hull (FDH) dependency-free efficiency frontier: district efficient peer group least well outcome strictly less spend. input-oriented score min(spend among peers outcome >= ) / spend. needs linear-programming solver makes functional-form assumption (unlike regression residual tges_efficiency()). Spend treated input (lower better) outcome output (higher better); pass outcome higher better (rate percentile). Cautions. Use one consistent per-pupil definition spend_col (CSG1 budgetary vs CSG1AA total), filter spend_df one calc_type, make sure outcome spend peer systems stated. frontier meaningful peer group: districts per group, land frontier default.

title: Spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (free-disposal hull) — tges_frontier
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  grate <- fetch_grad_rate(2023, methodology = "4 year") %>%   add_dfg() %>%   filter(dfg == "A", is_district, subgroup == "total") %>%   grate_percentile_rank(peer_type = "dfg")  spend <- fetch_tges(2024)$CSG1 %>%   filter(calc_type == "Actuals", end_year == 2023)  fr <- tges_frontier(spend, grate, outcome_col = "grad_rate_percentile",                     peer = "dfg") fr %>% filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(district_name, efficiency_score, reference_district_name, excess_spend) } # }

title: Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
text: Joins two TGES governance tables – CSG20 (budgeted general fund balance vs. actual) CSG21 (excess unreserved general fund balance) – one row per district-year, adds two flags board member needs budget vote: structural-deficit signal surplus--cap signal.

title: Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
text: tges Output fetch_tges() fetch_many_tges(). years Optional numeric vector. Keep end_year values.

title: Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
text: tibble entity columns, end_year,   budgeted_fund_balance, actual_fund_balance,   fund_balance_variance, excess_unreserved,   balance_yoy_change, two logical flags.

title: Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
text: Two failure modes, two flags: excess_surplus_flag: TRUE NJ DOE reports positive     excess unreserved balance (excess_unreserved > 0). NJ districts may     hold undesignated general fund surplus statutory cap (2     general fund budget, $250k greater); CSG21 reports amount     allowance, positive value surplus-hoarding signal. declining_balance_flag: TRUE actual general     fund balance fell year year (balance_yoy_change < 0). Computed     one year present district; reserves drawn     paper operating gaps structural-deficit signal. fund_balance_variance (actual - budgeted) reported context: large negative variance means district held far less surplus budgeted. flags descriptive summaries NJ DOE's reported figures, audit determinations. CSG20/CSG21 report end_year - 2 end_year - 1 guide; pass fetch_many_tges() output longer series.

title: Fund-balance health: budgeted vs actual and excess surplus — tges_fund_balance_health
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Newark fund-balance health over the available window tges_fund_balance_health(fetch_many_tges(2022:2024)) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, budgeted_fund_balance, actual_fund_balance,          excess_unreserved, excess_surplus_flag)  # Districts drawing down reserves in the latest year tges_fund_balance_health(fetch_tges(2024)) %>%   filter(declining_balance_flag) } # }

Higher metric value = higher percentile. Ranking computed within year_col peer column, also within indicator calc_type columns present, stacked multi-indicator / multi-calc-type frames rank correctly.

title: Percentile-rank a TGES metric within a peer group — tges_percentile_rank
text: df tidied TGES table (e.g. fetch_tges(2024)$CSG1) output tges_composition(). metric_col Character. Column rank. Default "Per Pupil costs". peer Character. Peer group: "tges_group" (default): TGES enrollment-band group     (network; matches set NJ's published rank uses) "dfg": District Factor Group (fetches DFG data network) "county": within county_name ("town next door" set) "statewide": districts "custom": caller-supplied set district codes (see     custom_ids); ranks within set . hook     tges_find_peers() output. year_col Character. Year column. Default "end_year". prefix Character. Prefix output columns. Default "peer". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision peer = "dfg". Default 2000. custom_ids Character vector district_ids. Required peer = "custom"; frame restricted codes ranked within . Ignored otherwise.

what: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
title: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
text: Per-pupil spend mechanically rises enrollment falls, fixed costs spread fewer students. function separates denominator artifact real cost growth, "costs exploding" can checked "simply fewer kids." important TGES data caution turned tool.

title: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
text: tges Output fetch_tges() fetch_many_tges(). years Optional numeric vector. Keep end_year values (applied differencing, lags still use adjacent years). deflator Optional data frame columns end_year price_index (real index supplied caller). present, adds real_total_exp, real_per_pupil, real_pp_growth.

title: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
text: tibble entity columns, end_year, total_exp,   ade, per_pupil, growth decomposition columns,   (deflator supplied) real-terms columns.

title: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
text: Built CSG1AA (total expenditures + average daily enrollment + per-pupil total). adjacent guides report overlapping actual years, rows de-duplicated district-year differencing. district-year (first), using identity \(\ln(pp) = \ln(exp) - \ln(ade)\): total_exp_growth, ade_growth, per_pupil_growth:     simple year--year percent changes. real_cost_component: \(\ln(exp_t / exp_{t-1})\),     spending-driven part per-pupil log growth. enrollment_component: \(-\ln(ade_t / ade_{t-1})\), part     driven purely changing denominator (positive enrollment falls). enrollment_effect_share: enrollment_component divided     total per-pupil log change – fraction per-pupil growth     enrollment artifact rather real spending. real price index supplied via deflator, function also returns inflation-adjusted total expenditure real_pp_growth. deflator fabricated: must pass real index (e.g. BLS CPI); without one, nominal decomposition returned.

title: Decompose per-pupil spending growth into real cost vs the enrollment effect — tges_real_growth
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  rg <- tges_real_growth(fetch_many_tges(2018:2024))  # Newark: how much of per-pupil growth is just falling enrollment? rg %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, per_pupil_growth, real_cost_component,          enrollment_component, enrollment_effect_share)  # With a real deflator (caller-supplied CPI), get real per-pupil growth cpi <- data.frame(end_year = 2018:2024,                   price_index = c(251.1, 255.7, 258.8, 271.0, 292.7, 304.7, 313.7)) tges_real_growth(fetch_many_tges(2018:2024), deflator = cpi) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, per_pupil_growth, real_pp_growth) } # }

Default "tges_group". year Numeric. Report year scan. Default: latest end_year present. calc_type Character. per-pupil cost tables, calc type rank. Default "Budgeted" (current-year budgeted figure). threshold Numeric. Decile width percentile points. Default 10: percentile <= 10 bottom-decile, >= 90 top-decile. only_flagged Logical. text: scan covers per-pupil cost tables (CSG1, CSG2, CSG6, CSG8, CSG8A, CSG10, CSG12, CSG13, CSG15) ranked "Per Pupil costs", plus classroom administration shares tges_composition(). row carries higher_means direction unambiguous: top-decile "Administration $/pupil" cost flag, top-decile "Classroom share" favourable. Percentile recomputed within chosen peer group (NJ's published enrollment-band rank, unless peer = "tges_group"), indicators use one consistent peer system.

title: Red-flag scan: where a district sits in the top or bottom decile of peers — tges_red_flags
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Newark's red flags within its enrollment-band peers tges_red_flags(fetch_tges(2024), district_id = "3570")  # Full profile within DFG A peers, nothing hidden tges_red_flags(fetch_tges(2024), district_id = "3570",                peer = "dfg", only_flagged = FALSE) } # }

what: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix
title: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix
text: Reshapes VITSTAT revenue shares one row per district-year revenue source share (0-1, source reports ) per-pupil-dollar attribution (share times total spending per pupil). taxpayer's whole question one table: much budget local property tax versus state aid versus one-time federal money. VITSTAT single-year table (reports end_year - 1 given guide), pass fetch_many_tges() output build revenue-mix series across years.

title: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix

title: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix
text: tibble entity columns, end_year, total_pp,   *_share columns (local, state, federal, tuition, free_balance,   ), matching *_pp per-pupil dollar columns.

title: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix
text: Shares NJ DOE's reported fractions guaranteed sum exactly 1 (rounding). per-pupil dollar columns (local_pp, state_pp, ...) total_pp multiplied share, carry rounding. total_pp VITSTAT's "Total Spending Per Pupil", equals CSG1AA per-pupil total expenditure year.

title: Decompose where a district's school dollars come from — tges_revenue_mix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Newark: what share is local property tax? tges_revenue_mix(fetch_tges(2024)) %>%   filter(district_id == "3570") %>%   select(end_year, total_pp, local_share, state_share, federal_share)  # The most local-tax-dependent districts in the latest year tges_revenue_mix(fetch_tges(2024)) %>%   arrange(desc(local_share)) %>%   select(district_name, local_share, local_pp) %>%   head(10) } # }

what: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
text: bumpy district's funding, fragile peers? chosen series computes per-district volatility across available years (coefficient variation plus typical worst year--year swing) ranks within peer group. Pointed federal-revenue share quantitative companion ESSER-cliff screen; pointed total per-pupil spend flags districts whose budgets lurch year year.

title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
code: tges_volatility(   tges,   metric = "total_pp",   peer = c("tges_group", "dfg", "county", "statewide"),   min_years = 3,   table = "CSG1",   calc_type = "Budgeted",   dfg_revision = 2000 )

title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
text: tges Output fetch_many_tges() (multi-year). metric Character. Series measure. Default "total_pp". peer Character. Peer group. One "tges_group" (default), "dfg", "county", "statewide". min_years Integer. Minimum finite observations per district. Default 3. table Character. Per-pupil cost table source metric neither revenue-mix composition column. Default "CSG1". calc_type Character. Calc type composition/table sources. Default "Budgeted". dfg_revision Numeric. DFG revision peer = "dfg". Default 2000.

title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
text: per-district tibble: entity columns, peer_group,   n_years, mean_value, sd_value, cv,   mean_abs_yoy, max_abs_yoy, rank columns   vol_rank, vol_n, vol_percentile.

title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
text: Needs multi-year input (fetch_many_tges()). metric pulled tges_revenue_mix() (e.g. total_pp, federal_share, local_share), else tges_composition() (e.g. classroom_share), else per-pupil cost table column. Districts fewer min_years finite observations dropped. cv sd / |mean|; mean_abs_yoy max_abs_yoy mean max absolute year--year percent change. volatility rank computed cv within peer group (higher cv = higher percentile = volatile).

title: Year-to-year funding volatility, ranked against peers — tges_volatility
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(dplyr)  # Whose federal-revenue share whipsawed the most through the ESSER years? tges_volatility(fetch_many_tges(2018:2025), metric = "federal_share",                 peer = "dfg") %>%   arrange(desc(vol_percentile)) %>%   select(district_name, mean_value, cv, max_abs_yoy, vol_percentile) %>%   head(10) } # }

title: tidy Total Spending Detail — tidy_total_spending_detail
text: Cleans Total Spending Detail workbooks (Detail_FY##.xlsx) ship inside 2024+ TGES bundles.  break district's Total Spending Per Pupil six components , summed, equal published per-pupil total: General Current Expense, Capital Outlay, Grants & Entitlements, Food Services, Debt Service locally issued bonds, Debt Service School Development Authority (SDA) bonds. Unlike budget indicators (CSG1-15), per-pupil amounts divided daily enrollment plus sent pupils, resident enrollment, directly comparable CSG1's budgetary per-pupil cost districts educate pupils.  tges_excluded_costs() carries enrollment denominator flags sending districts reason. data year taken FY## token file name (2025 guide ships Detail_FY24 = end_year 2024 Detail_FY23 = end_year 2023), report end_year.

what: Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
title: Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
text: Tracks ESSA accountability status changes across multiple years, identifying improvement trajectories, calculating transition probabilities, summarizing patterns school accountability status.

title: Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
text: df_list named list data frames different years. element named end_year (e.g., list("2020" = df_2020, "2024" = df_2024)). Data frames fetch_essa_status. school_id Optional school code track specific school (e.g., "010")

title: Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
text: List two elements: longitudinal - Data frame one row per school-year combination: end_year - School year county_id, district_id, school_id - Location identifiers school_name - School name category_of_identification - ESSA status category focus_level - Categorized support level (Comprehensive/Targeted//None) status_change - Change previous year:           "Improvement", "Decline", "Stable", "First Year", "Insufficient Data" transitions - Data frame transition summary statistics: from_status - Status previous year to_status - Status current year n_schools - Number schools transition pct_schools - Percentage transitions summary - List summary statistics: n_schools_tracked - Total unique schools tracked n_years - Number years data n_improvements - Number schools showing improvement n_declines - Number schools showing decline

title: Track ESSA Progress Over Time — track_essa_progress_over_time
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fetch data for multiple years essa_2020 <- fetch_essa_status(2020) essa_2022 <- fetch_essa_status(2022) essa_2024 <- fetch_essa_status(2024)  # Combine into named list df_list <- list(   "2020" = essa_2020,   "2022" = essa_2022,   "2024" = essa_2024 )  # Track progress over time progress <- track_essa_progress_over_time(df_list)  # View longitudinal data head(progress$longitudinal)  # View transition patterns progress$transitions  # View summary progress$summary  # Track specific school single_school <- track_essa_progress_over_time(df_list, school_id = "010") } # }

title: njschooldata 0.9.11
text: New vignette, “Head First Analyst’s Guide NJ TGES” (also dev-docs/tges-analyst-guide.md). Ten common analytical traps Taxpayers’ Guide Education Spending avoid : three different “per pupil” numbers, resident-vs-sent-pupil denominator breaks cross-district subtraction, county special-services outliers, nominal vs real dollars, actuals vs budgeted years, within-peer-group ranks, ESSER cliff inside Total Spending, state aid predict spending, guide-year vs end_year mismatch, average-row / district-code hygiene. points relevant analysis helper.

title: njschooldata 0.9.10
text: Category names normalized across years, label drift source (“Choice Aid” vs “School Choice Aid”; “Special Education Categorical Aid” vs “Special Education Aid”) collapses one cross-year name. fetcher tries current-year direct workbook URL first, falls back archived per-year zip bundle locates district-details member name (also drifts across years). Valid years 2019 later. Note: transportation_aid formula subsidy, transportation cost, runs well district’s actual spending; see TGES dev-doc.

title: njschooldata 0.9.9
text: Total Spending Detail now parsed. Detail_FY##.xlsx workbooks ship inside 2024+ TGES bundles passing un-tidied (real header sits row 3 description banner). get_raw_tges() now skips banner tidy_total_spending_detail() cleans six per-pupil component columns (general current expense, capital outlay, grants & entitlements, food service, locally-issued debt service, SDA debt service) sum published total. Tables surface DETAIL_FY24 / DETAIL_FY23 fetch_tges() output. tges_excluded_costs() joins Total Spending Detail CSG1 expose, per district-year, everything budgetary per-pupil figure leaves , including state-paid -behalf TPAF pension. returns six components plus two differences: excluded_total_pp (total spending minus budgetary, full wedge) gce_excess_pp (general current expense minus budgetary, roughly transportation + -behalf TPAF + tuition + judgments). budgetary cost divides resident enrollment Detail figures divide enrollment plus sent pupils, helper carries sent_pupil_share residual_reliable flag sending districts (per-pupil subtraction breaks ) marked. Neither difference isolates pension ; public TGES file breaks -behalf TPAF line.

title: njschooldata 0.9.8
text: Taxpayers’ Guide Educational Spending (TGES) fetchers work . NJ DOE retired old state.nj.us/education/guide/{year}/ URLs (every one now returns 404) moved files nj.gov/education/guide/docs/. fetch_tges() get_raw_tges() build current URLs cover 2001-2025, adding 2020-2025 (including 2024/2025 per-year bundle layout). 1999 2000 dropped NJ links downloads 404 source.

what: Other TGES parsing fixes (surfaced while restoring the fetcher)
title: njschooldata 0.9.8
text: District ranks longer vanish. 2019 , ranks ship “rank|out_of” (e.g. “33|57”) coerced -NA; now parsed integer rank via parse_rank(). Personnel tables (CSG16-19) longer emit duplicate columns. year mask mis-split modern 4-digit codes (strat0016/strat0116) CSG19’s farat01/farat02 suffixes. Budget tables (CSG3/7/9/11) keep percentage columns distinct (cost share total budget vs. salaries) instead collapsing one. CSG14 (employee benefits share salaries) reshapes full 3-year window instead squeezed 2-year personnel layout. tidy_vitstat() returns spending, revenue mix, ratios numbers rather character. Missing-data markers (“N.R.” Reported, “N..” Applicable) coerce cleanly NA without warnings.

title: njschooldata 0.9.8
text: tges_composition() reshapes per-category indicators one row per district-year category per-pupil dollar plus share budgetary per-pupil cost. tges_percentile_rank() ranks TGES metric within peer group (TGES enrollment band, DFG, county, statewide). tges_efficiency() joins per-pupil spend caller-supplied outcome percentile labels spend-vs-outcome quadrant. tges_revenue_mix() decomposes VITSTAT revenue shares per-pupil dollar attribution (local property tax vs. state aid vs. federal). tges_fund_balance_health() joins CSG20/CSG21 flags structural deficit (declining actual balance) excess surplus statutory cap. tges_federal_exposure() screens ESSER cliff federal revenue share: pre-pandemic baseline vs. ESSER-window peak, flagged per-pupil spending grew surge. tges_staffing() reshapes CSG16-19 CSG14 negotiation dashboard (student/teacher, student/administrator, faculty/administrator ratios, median salaries, benefits share salaries). tges_red_flags() loops rank wrapper across every major indicator surfaces district’s top/bottom-decile placements within peers. tges_real_growth() decomposes per-pupil spending growth real-cost component enrollment (denominator) component sum total, optional caller-supplied price deflator real-terms growth. tges_find_peers() builds data-driven peer set scaled Euclidean distance enrollment, per-pupil cost, composition, revenue mix, feeds new peer = "custom" mode tges_percentile_rank(). tges_frontier() scores district free-disposal-hull spend-versus-outcome efficiency frontier (0-1) names district reaches least outcome less money (solver dependency). tges_convergence() regresses spending growth starting level within peer group test beta-convergence (gaps closing widening?). tges_composition_drift() measures spending share moved two years ranks move peers. tges_gap_cost() translates peer gap (e.g. classroom share vs. DFG median) per-pupil district-wide dollars. tges_volatility() measures year--year funding volatility (coefficient variation plus typical/worst swing) ranks within peer group. tges_compare() assembles side--side fiscal scorecard named set districts (counterfactual-cities table).

title: njschooldata 0.9.8
text: New “Following Money” spending deep-dives built entirely fetch_tges(): South Orange-Maplewood (property-tax suburb) Newark (Abbott district state aid funds ~80% budget). traces 25 years per-pupil spending, revenue mix, benefits, facilities, classroom dollar; Newark article also compares district largest charter networks. “Newark’s Gains Cost? DFG Fiscal Brief” applies comparative toolkit benchmark Newark 37 highest-need DFG peers revenue mix, classroom share, real vs. enrollment-driven cost growth, ESSER exposure, staffing, one-page red-flag scan. “Newark Efficiency Frontier: Dollars Pay ?” works new cross-district layer end end: data-driven peers, spend-versus-graduation efficiency frontier, dollar cost closing classroom-share gap, DFG spending convergence, composition drift, federal-funding volatility, five-city scorecard.

title: njschooldata 0.9.8
text: Added comprehensive TGES test suite: unit tests URL builder rank parser plus live ground-truth round-trip fidelity tests pin real district values verify wide--long reshape neither invents drops rows across full 2001-2025 range. comparative toolkit covered test-tges-analysis.R (synthetic-fixture unit tests reshape/rank/join/decomposition math plus live integration). cross-district layer adds 34 cases pin free-disposal-hull scores references, convergence/divergence signs, signed composition drift, share--dollars gap math, volatility ranking, scorecard assembly, live checks 2024 guide DFG graduation outcomes.

title: njschooldata 0.9.7
text: New School Performance Report (SPR) fetchers redesigned 2024-25 databases: ESSA accountability: fetch_spr_essa_targets() (six long-term-goal indicators), fetch_spr_accountability_summative(), fetch_spr_tsi(), fetch_spr_essa_status_counts(), fixed district-level fetch_essa_status(). Graduation, language, assessment: fetch_spr_grad_pathways(), fetch_spr_home_language(), fetch_spr_naep(). Staff: fetch_spr_admin_experience(), fetch_spr_staff_counts(), fetch_spr_staff_demo_subject(), fetch_spr_staff_education(), fetch_spr_staff_retention(), fetch_spr_teacher_exp_subject(), fetch_spr_educator_equity(). Historical coverage: 2024-25 SPR fetchers extended backward earliest year source sheet exists structure maps redesigned shape without fabrication (e.g. fetch_spr_home_language() fetch_spr_staff_education() 2018, fetch_spr_naep() 2017, fetch_spr_grad_pathways() across 2018-2022 2024). Years underlying sheet absent reports different measure error explanatory message rather guessing mapping. fetch_sgp() (median Student Growth Percentiles) now supports pre-2025 years: type = "by_grade" type = "trends" back 2018, type = "by_performance_level" 2023. SY2019-20 SY2021-22 remain unavailable (NJ produced SGP COVID assessment pause), pre-2020 -performance-level sheet (growth-band percentage distribution, median SGP) stays gated. Pre-2025 trends rows preserve legacy MetTarget flag new ela_met_target / math_met_target columns.

title: njschooldata 0.9.7
text: SPR Excel workbooks now cached disk (per year + level), workbook downloaded NJ DOE reused across sheet reads across sessions – reading second sheet 2024-25 District file drops ~12s ~0.1s. New helpers: njsd_workbook_cache_dir(), njsd_workbook_cache_info(), njsd_workbook_cache_clear(). Relocate cache options(njschooldata.cache_dir =) disable options(njschooldata.workbook_cache = FALSE). Downloads validated real .xlsx files (ZIP signature) caching, HTTP error bot-protection page never cached parsed data.

title: njschooldata 0.9.6
text: Live geocoding enrich_school_latlong(use_cache = FALSE) now uses CRAN package tidygeocoder instead GitHub-, CRAN-archived placement package. new path cascades keyless US Census geocoder OpenStreetMap (Nominatim), optional Google pass api_key supplied. removes Remotes: DerekYves/placement entry DESCRIPTION, dependency resolution longer touches GitHub remote (intermittently failing CI “Bad GitHub credentials”). default cached path (use_cache = TRUE, bundled geocoded_cached dataset, built tidygeocoder) unchanged.

title: njschooldata 0.9.5
text: fetch_disciplinary_removals() now requests correct discipline-removals sheet every supported year. sheet renamed several times NJ DOE Database_SchoolDetail workbooks: DisciplinaryRemovals end_years 2018-2023, DisciplinaryRemovalsByStudgroup end_year 2024, RemovalsStudentGroupGrade end_year 2025+. Previously function asked DisciplinaryRemovalsByStudgroup 2017-2024, returned data 2018-2023 (sheet exist years). SY2016-17 (end_year 2017) discipline-removals sheet .

title: njschooldata 0.9.5
text: range, since 2025 now valid. SPR tests: list_spr_sheets() returns plain character vector, list_spr_sheets tests now use expect_type(x, "character") instead incorrect expect_s3_class(x, "character") (character vector S3 class, old assertion always failed run online). SPR tests: replaced misspelled, nonexistent "GraduatonRateTrendsProgress" sheet confirmed "GraduationRateTrendsProgress" sheet 2023-24 workbook. Fixed self-comparison track_essa_progress_over_time(): school_id filter used dplyr::filter(school_id == school_id), data-masked sides column ignored argument. now uses .env$school_id, new offline unit test covers filtered unfiltered paths. Hardened SPR test suite skip_on_cran() + skip_if_offline() guards every networked block suite degrades gracefully offline.

title: njschooldata 0.9.4
text: New fetch_sgp() fetches NJ Student Growth Percentile (median SGP / mSGP) data redesigned 2024-25 SPR databases. Three type options map three source sheets: "trends" (StudentGrowthTrends, median SGP student group ELA Math, statewide comparison columns), "by_grade" (StudentGrowthbyGrade, subject grade), "by_performance_level" (StudentGrowthByPerformLevel, subject prior NJSLA performance level). entity median normalized level-agnostic *_median_sgp column (school value level = "school", district value level = "district"), suppressed cells (“Fewer 10 testers”) mapped NA reason retained companion *_category column. end_year = 2025 supported now; pre-2025 SPR databases store SGP differently-shaped, differently-named sheets, documented follow-.

title: njschooldata 0.9.4
text: fetch_essa_status(end_year, level = "district") works SY2024-25 (end_year = 2025). 2024-25 redesign removed ESSAAccountabilityStatus sheet District/State workbook replaced ESSAAccountabilityStatusList (per-entity status, structural analogue legacy sheet) ESSAAccountabilityStatusCounts (aggregate CSI/ATSI/TSI tallies). District-level 2025+ requests now map ESSAAccountabilityStatusList. School-level requests continue use ESSAAccountabilityStatus years, pre-2025 behavior unchanged.

title: njschooldata 0.9.3
text: School Performance Reports (SPR) fetchers now support SY2024-25 (end_year 2025). NJ DOE heavily restructured 2024-25 SPR workbooks: column header row moved row 1 row 4, ~10 source sheets renamed, many value columns gained _School/_State suffixes. get_spr_url() accepts 2017-2025, fetch_spr_data() skips three preamble rows 2025+. Sheet renames handled per source 2025 keeping 2017-2024 behavior intact: chronic absenteeism (ChronicAbsenteeismStudentGroup), chronic absenteeism grade (ChronicAbsenteeismGrade), violence/vandalism/HIB (IncidentsbyType), SAT/ACT/PSAT participation (PSATSATACT_Participation), SAT/ACT/PSAT performance (joined PSATSATACT_AverageScore + PSATSATACT_Benchmark), CTE (CTEParticipants_* columns), industry credentials (IndustryValuedCredClusters), work-based learning (WorkBasedLearning), social studies (SocStudiesCoursePart), world languages (WorldLanguagesCoursePart), computer science (CompSciITCoursePart), visual/performing arts (VisualPerformingArts), seal biliteracy (SealofBiliteracy_Language), ESSA progress (ESSAAccountabilityTrends). SAT participation SAT performance 2025 sheets now multi-year trend tables; fetchers filter requested academic year via new internal filter_spr_to_year() helper output keeps historical single-year, one-row-per-school shape. clean_spr_subgroups() maps new 2024-25 “Students” total label total population, SPR statewide aggregate row (CDS code State/State) now correctly flagged is_state. Assessment fetchers (fetch_parcc()/NJSLA, fetch_njgpa(), fetch_access(), plus fetch_all_parcc()/fetch_all_njgpa()/fetch_all_access()) now cover SY2024-25 (end_year = 2025). NJ DOE reverted 2019-era space-encoded filenames 2025 (e.g. ELA03%20NJSLA%20DATA%202024-25.xlsx); URL builders now use spaces 2019 2025+ underscores 2022-2024. PARCC_VALID_YEARS extends 2025. Schemas unchanged 2024. Extend 4-year 6-year graduation fetchers SY2024-25 (end_year 2025). fetch_grad_rate(2025, "4 year"), fetch_grad_count(2025), fetch_6yr_grad_rate(2025) (school district) now work. NJ DOE restructured SY2024-25 files: 4-year ACGR file (Cohort2025) renamed Graduation Rate -> Adjusted Cohort Graduation Rate Cohort Count -> Adjusted Cohort Count; SPR 6-year cohort data moved 6YrGraduationCohortProfile sheet combined GraduationCohortProfile sheet (filtered CohortType == "6-Year", header row 4, _School/_District/_State column suffixes, percent-string rate values). Subgroup labels renamed NJ DOE (Total -> Students, Hispanic -> Hispanic/Latino) normalized back package’s standard names cross-year filters keep working.

title: njschooldata 0.9.3
text: fetch_disciplinary_removals() broken every year: requested sheet "DisciplinaryRemovals" never existed. now selects real sheet per year (DisciplinaryRemovalsByStudgroup 2017-2024, RemovalsStudentGroupGrade 2025) standardizes student-group/grade column student_group_grade. fetch_apprenticeship_data() longer errors year-column rename (dplyr::rename() mapping inverted, naming columns exist); rename now correctly directed skipped columns absent. fetch_spr_data() drops trailing "end worksheet" sentinel row 2024-25 sheets append. get_raw_sla() now maps Geometry math test code GEO GEO01, NJ DOE used since 2022. old gsub("ALG", "ALG0", ...) step left GEO unchanged, fetch_parcc(year, "GEO", "math") silently 404’d 2022-2024. Geometry results now fetch 2022-2025. fetch_6yr_grad_rate() longer fabricates suppressed-district rates. SPR cohort profiles repeat statewide reference rate State:/_State columns every district row, 2025 district path filled onto ordinary districts whose rate blank (suppressed, fewer 10 students) via unconditional coalesce(..._District, ..._State). 2024-25 assigned statewide subgroup rate ~2,700 suppressed district-subgroup rows. fallback State:/_State columns now restricted statewide aggregate row, suppressed districts stay NA. fetch_6yr_grad_rate() now flags statewide aggregate row is_state 2017-2024 well, populates row’s rate. Every SPR cohort profile stores literal string "State" statewide row’s CountyCode/DistrictCode instead numeric 99/9999 codes; normalization added 2025 now runs years, legacy district path fills statewide row’s rate State: columns, statewide 6-year rate longer silently dropped older years.

title: njschooldata 0.9.3
text: fetch_ap_participation() yet available 2025. 2024-25 AP_IB_Dual_Participation SPR sheet malformed school level (many rows per school disambiguating column), reliable one-row-per-school mapping derived without fabricating data. 2025 path raises informative error; 2017-2024 unaffected.

title: njschooldata 0.9.2
text: fetch_enr() now works end--end 1999-2009. Pre-2010 NJDOE files arrive combined “01-ATLANTIC” strings COUNTY / DISTRICT / SCHOOL columns. clean_enr_names() renames county_name / district_name / school_name, split_enr_cols() creates matching *_id columns splitting "-". process_enr() previously called dplyr::mutate(county_id = ...) cleanup step split_enr_cols(), every pre-2010 year errored “object ‘county_id’ found.” Reordered split_enr_cols() runs first. ENR_VALID_YEARS extended back 1999. earlier note “1999 removed NJ DOE website” turned stale; 1998-99 ZIP still hosted /education/doedata/enr/enr99/enrollment_9899.zip parses cleanly fix .

title: njschooldata 0.9.2
text: full enrollment panel now 1999-2026 (28 years). State Hispanic enrollment grew every single year 1999-2025 posted first decline (-6,318, -1.3%) 2025-26.

title: njschooldata 0.9.1
text: fetch_enr() now supports 2026 (2025-26 fall enrollment). ENR_VALID_YEARS enrollment year_ranges extend 2026. get_raw_enr() resilient NJ DOE’s filename capitalization: 2025-26 file shipped Enrollment_2526.zip (capital E) versus historical lowercase enrollment_*.zip. fetcher now tries .

title: njschooldata 0.9.1
text: Fixed fetch_grad_rate() years. 2026 NJ DOE retired /schoolperformance/grad/ tree moved every cohort file /spr/adddata/doc/acgrdocs/; old URLs began returning 404 HTML page failed open xlsx. 4-year (2011-2024) 5-year (2012-2019) URLs now point current location. Fixed long-standing state-level grade-8 dropout. NJ DOE ships label “Eight Grade” (sic) row value State worksheet; existing typo fix corrected column names, state 8th-grade enrollment (~100k students) silently landed NA-grade row 2020+ years. State grade-8 totals now map “08” match sum district grade-8 totals. State district/school totals unchanged.

title: njschooldata 0.9.1
text: 2026 K-12 enrollment fell 1.90% (state total 1.72%) 2025 - first real decline years. Verified genuine demographic change: cohort retention 2025->2026 sits [0.975, 1.013] every grade, PreK rose K-12 fell, state total reproduces NJ DOE’s published 1,357,450 exactly. definition coverage change.

title: njschooldata 0.9.1
text: Extended enrollment year-coverage tests 2026 pins added structural invariants (state = sum districts, PK + K12UG = total, state grade-8 regression, cohort-retention believability). Fixed stale test_validation.R assertions expected 1999 enrollment valid (1999 removed NJ DOE website; range starts 2000).

title: njschooldata 0.9.1
text: Refreshed enrollment vignette README 2020-2026 window, 15 stories recomputed committed figure-html charts regenerated published site reflects 2026. Plot chunks now default cache = FALSE prevent stale cached figures.

title: njschooldata 0.9.0
text: Extended data support 2024-2025 school year fetch_enr() supports 2024 2025 data fetch_parcc() supports 2024 NJSLA data Added GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows
